paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: For seven decades, Georgia O’Keeffe (1887–1986) was a major figure in American art. Remarkably, she remained independent from shifting art trends and her work stayed true to her own vision, which was based on finding the essential, abstract forms in nature. With exceptionally keen powers of observation and great finesse with a paintbrush, she recorded subtle nuances of colour, shape, and light that enlivened her paintings and attracted a wide audience.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Born in 1887 near Sun Prairie, Wisconsin to cattle breeders Francis and Ida O’Keeffe, Georgia was raised on their farm along with her six siblings. By the time she graduated from high school in 1905, she had determined to make her way as an artist. She studied the techniques of traditional painting at the Art Institute of Chicago school (1905) and the Art Students League of New York (1907–8). After attending university and then training college, she became an art teacher and taught in elementary schools, high schools, and colleges in Virginia, Texas, and South Carolina from 1911 to 1918.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: During this period, O’Keeffe began to experiment with creating abstract compositions in charcoal, and produced a series of innovative drawings that led her art in a new direction. She sent some of these drawings to a friend in New York, who showed them to art collector and photographer Alfred Stieglitz in January 1916. Stieglitz was impressed, and exhibited the drawings later that year at his gallery on Fifth Avenue, New York City, where the works of many avant-garde artists and photographers were introduced to the American public.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: With Stieglitz’s encouragement and promise of financial support, O’Keeffe arrived in New York in June 1918 to begin a career as an artist. For the next three decades, Stieglitz vigorously promoted her work in twenty-two solo exhibitions and numerous group installations. The two were married in 1924. The ups and downs of their personal and professional relationship were recorded in Stieglitz’s celebrated black-and-white portraits of O’Keeffe, taken over the course of twenty years (1917–37).

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: By the mid-1920s, O’Keeffe was recognized as one of America’s most important and successful artists, widely known for the architectural pictures that dramatically depict the soaring skyscrapers of New York. But most often, she painted botanical subjects, inspired by annual trips to the Stieglitz family summer home. In her magnified images depicting flowers, begun in 1924, O’Keeffe brings the viewer right into the picture.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Enlarging the tiniest details to fill an entire metre-wide canvas emphasized their shapes and lines and made them appear abstract. Such daring compositions helped establish O’Keeffe’s reputation as an innovative modernist.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: In 1929, O’Keeffe made her first extended trip to the state of New Mexico. It was a visit that had a lasting impact on her life, and an immediate effect on her work. Over the next two decades she made almost annual trips to New Mexico, staying up to six months there, painting in relative solitude, then returning to New York each winter to exhibit the new work at Stieglitz’s gallery. This pattern continued until she moved permanently to New Mexico in 1949.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: There, O’Keeffe found new inspiration: at first, it was the numerous sun-bleached bones she came across in the state’s rugged terrain that sparked her imagination. Two of her earliest and most celebrated Southwestern paintings exquisitely reproduce a cow skull’s weathered surfaces, jagged edges, and irregular openings. Later, she also explored another variation on this theme in her large series of Pelvis pictures, which focused on the contrasts between convex and concave surfaces, and solid and open spaces.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: However, it was the region’s spectacular landscape, with its unusual geological formations, vivid colours, clarity of light, and exotic vegetation, that held the artist’s imagination for more than four decades. Often, she painted the rocks, cliffs, and mountains in striking close-up, just as she had done with her botanical subjects.

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: O’Keeffe eventually owned two homes in New Mexico – the first, her summer retreat at Ghost Ranch, was nestled beneath 200-metre cliffs, while the second, used as her winter residence, was in the small town of Abiquiú. While both locales provided a wealth of imagery for her paintings, one feature of the Abiquiú house – the large walled patio with its black door – was particularly inspirational. In more than thirty pictures between 1946 and 1960, she reinvented the patio into an abstract arrangement of geometric shapes.

paragraphs.label: K
paragraphs.text: From the 1950s into the 1970s, O’Keeffe travelled widely, making trips to Asia, the Middle East, and Europe. Flying in planes inspired her last two major series – aerial views of rivers and expansive paintings of the sky viewed from just above clouds. In both series, O’Keeffe increased the size of her canvases, sometimes to mural proportions, reflecting perhaps her newly expanded view of the world. When in 1965 she successfully translated one of her cloud motifs to a monumental canvas measuring 6 metres in length (with the help of assistants), it was an enormous challenge and a special feat for an artist nearing eighty years of age.

paragraphs.label: L
paragraphs.text: The last two decades of the artist’s life were relatively unproductive as ill health and blindness hindered her ability to work. O’Keeffe died in 1986 at the age of ninety-eight, but her rich legacy of some 900 paintings has continued to attract subsequent generations of artists and art lovers who derive inspiration from these very American images.

 3. **答案句理解**：题干中“painted pictures of clouds and 【7】 seen from above”表明需要两个并列的绘画主题，其中“clouds”对应原文“expansive paintings of the sky viewed from just above clouds”。原文用“and”连接另一个主题“aerial views of rivers”，“aerial views”（空中视角）即“seen from above”，因此“rivers”是与“clouds”并列的、从上方看到并绘画的事物，即为答案。

questions.title: Georgia O'Keeffe's style was greatly influenced by the changing fashions in art over the seven decades of her career.
questions.analysis:
1. **题目定位**：首先，我们阅读题干，明确题目是关于Georgia O'Keeffe的艺术风格是否在其七十年的职业生涯中受到艺术界变化潮流的极大影响。
 2. **寻找关键词**：从题干中提取关键信息点："Georgia O'Keeffe's style"（奥基夫的风格）、"was greatly influenced by"（被极大地影响）、"changing fashions in art"（变化的艺术时尚/潮流）以及"over the seven decades of her career"（在她七十年的职业生涯中）。
 3. **定位原文答案句**：带着这些关键词浏览文章，很快在文章第一段第二句找到关键信息："Remarkably, she remained independent from shifting art trends and her work stayed true to her own vision, which was based on finding the essential, abstract forms in nature."
 4. **理解答案句**：这句话的核心意思是奥基夫“始终独立于不断变化的艺术潮流”（remained independent from shifting art trends），并且她的作品“忠于她自己的艺术视野”（her work stayed true to her own vision）。
 5. **对比题干与原文**：题干声称她的风格“被极大地影响”（was greatly influenced by）“变化的艺术时尚”（changing fashions in art）。而原文明确指出她“独立于”（remained independent from）“变化的艺术潮流”（shifting art trends）。“被极大影响”和“保持独立/不受影响”是完全相反的概念。
 6. **得出结论**：由于题干的陈述与原文提供的信息直接矛盾，因此答案为FALSE。

questions.title: O'Keeffe produced a greater quantity of work during the 1950s to 1970s than at any other time in her life.
questions.analysis:
第一步：题目定位。题干核心是判断“O'Keeffe在1950-1970年代创作的作品数量比她生命中任何其他时期都多”，因此需先定位到文章中关于“1950-1970年代”的内容。通过线索词“during the 1950s to 1970s”，可快速定位到原文倒数第二段开头“From the 1950s into the 1970s...”。
 第二步：寻找关键词对应。题干中的“O'Keeffe”对应原文的“O’Keeffe”；“produced”对应原文中描述创作行为的“inspired her last two major series”“painted”“translated one of her cloud motifs to a monumental canvas”；“during the 1950s to 1970s”直接对应“From the 1950s into the 1970s”。但题干中关键的比较信息“a greater quantity of work”（作品数量更多）和“than at any other time in her life”（比生命中任何其他时期）在原文中未找到对应线索词。
 第三步：理解答案句。定位段落主要描述了欧姬芙在1950-1970年代的旅行经历、创作灵感来源（飞行）、作品主题（河流鸟瞰图、天空画作）、画布尺寸变化（增大到壁画比例）以及具体创作案例（6米长云纹画布），但未提及该时期的“作品数量”，更未将此时期的作品数量与她“生命中任何其他时期”进行比较。

central_sentences.sentence: From the 1950s into the 1970s, O’Keeffe travelled widely, making trips to Asia, the Middle East, and Europe. Flying in planes inspired her last two major series – aerial views of rivers and expansive paintings of the sky viewed from just above clouds. In both series, O’Keeffe increased the size of her canvases, sometimes to mural proportions, reflecting perhaps her newly expanded view of the world. When in 1965 she successfully translated one of her cloud motifs to a monumental canvas measuring 6 metres in length (with the help of assistants), it was an enormous challenge and a special feat for an artist nearing eighty years of age.
central_sentences.translation: 从20世纪50年代到70年代，欧姬芙广泛旅行，曾前往亚洲、中东和欧洲。乘坐飞机激发了她最后两个主要系列作品的创作——河流的鸟瞰图和从云层正上方俯瞰天空的宏大画作。在这两个系列中，欧姬芙增大了画布的尺寸，有时达到壁画的比例，这或许反映了她对世界新的开阔视野。1965年，她在助手的帮助下，成功将自己的一个云纹图案绘制在一幅长达6米的巨大画布上，对于一位年近八旬的艺术家来说，这是一项巨大的挑战和非凡的成就。

content.title: Adapting to the effects of climate change
content.summary: {'A': '全球各国已在为气候变化及其影响做准备和适应，即便明天停止所有二氧化碳排放，工业时代以来已排放的二氧化碳仍会持续影响约40年，部分地区受影响更严重，这些地方创新活跃。', 'B': '美国佛罗里达州迈阿密海滩海水倒灌且从地下渗出，该市通过抬高道路和设置新水泵开展雨水管理项目来应对洪水，且未来还需根据环境变化进行调整。', 'C': '印度尼西亚爪哇岛北岸海堤常坍塌加剧海岸侵蚀，此前多数红树林种植项目失败，湿地国际组织采用建造半透坝的新方法，在沉降较小地区取得一定成功，强调应转向多功能方法。', 'D': '越南湄公河三角洲的浮力基金会项目自2006年起研究和改造两栖房屋，这种房屋在洪水时可漂浮，成本低、能避免风灾且可适应文化需求。', 'E': '孟加拉国易受气候变化影响，国际发展组织ICCO合作组织与当地农民合作，种植天然耐盐作物以适应盐渍土壤，使农民每年多收获两到三次。', 'F': '美国洛杉矶街道服务部门的首席可持续发展官领导“凉爽街道洛杉矶”项目，通过植树和安装“凉爽路面”系统来降低城市平均温度，部分街道已取得降温效果。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: All around the world, nations are already preparing for, and adapting to, climate change and its impacts. Even if we stopped all CO2 emissions tomorrow, we would continue to see the impact of the CO2 already released since industrial times, with scientists forecasting that global warming would continue for around 40 years. In the meantime, ice caps would continue to melt and sea levels rise. Some countries and regions will suffer more extreme impacts from these changes than others. It’s in these places that innovation is thriving.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: In Miami Beach, Florida, USA, seawater isn’t just breaching the island city’s walls, it’s seeping up through the ground, so the only way to save the city is to lift it up above sea level. Starting in the lowest and most vulnerable neighbourhoods, roads have been raised by as much as 61 centimetres. The elevation work was carried out as part of Miami Beach’s ambitious but much-needed stormwater-management programme. In addition to the road adaptations, the city has set up new pumps that can remove up to 75,000 litres of water per minute. In the face of floods, climate-mitigation strategies have often been overlooked, says Yanira Pineda, a senior sustainability coordinator. She knows that they’re essential and that the job is far from over. ‘We know that in 20, 30, 40 years, we'll need to go back in there and adjust to the changing environment,’ she says.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Seawalls are a staple strategy for many coastal communities, but on the soft, muddy northern shores of Java, Indonesia, they frequently collapse, further exacerbating coastal erosion. There have been many attempts to restore the island’s coastal mangroves: ecosystems of trees and shrubs that help defend coastal areas by trapping sediment in their net-like root systems, elevating the sea bed and dampening the energy of waves and tidal currents. But Susanna Tol of the not-for-profit organisation Wetlands International says that, while hugely popular, the majority of mangrove-planting projects fail. So, Wetlands International started out with a different approach, building semi-permeable dams, made from bamboo poles and brushwood, to mimic the role of mangrove roots and create favourable conditions for mangroves to grow back naturally. The programme has seen moderate success, mainly in areas with less subsidence. ‘Unfortunately, traditional infrastructure is often single-solution focused,’ says Tol. ‘For long-term success, it’s critical that we transition towards multifunctional approaches that embed natural processes and that engage and benefit communities and local decision-makers.’

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: As the floodwaters rose in the rice fields of the Mekong Delta in September 2018, four small houses rose with them. Homes in this part of Vietnam are traditionally built on stilts but these ones had been built to float. The modifications were made by the Buoyant Foundation Project, a not-for-profit organisation that has been researching and retrofitting amphibious houses since 2006. ‘When I started this,’ explains founder Elizabeth English, ‘climate change was not on the tip of everybody’s tongue, but this technology is becoming necessary in places that didn’t previously need it.’ It’s much cheaper than permanently elevating houses, English explains – about a third of what it would cost to completely replace a building’s foundations. It also avoids the problem of taller houses being at greater risk from wind damage. Another plus comes from the fact that amphibious structures can be sensitively adapted to meet cultural needs and match the kind of houses that are already common in a community.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Bangladesh is especially vulnerable to climate change. Most of the country is less than a metre above sea level and 80 per cent of its land lies on floodplains. ‘Almost 35 million people living on the coastal belt of Bangladesh are currently affected by soil and water salinity,’ says Raisa Chowdhury of the international development organisation ICCO Cooperation. Rather than fighting against it, one project is helping communities adapt to salt-affected soils. ICCO Cooperation has been working with 10,000 farmers in Bangladesh to start cultivating naturally salt-tolerant crops in the region. Certain varieties of carrot, potato, kohlrabi, cabbage and beetroot have been found to be better suited to salty soil than the rice and wheat that is typically grown there. Chowdhury says that the results are very visible, comparing a barren plot of land to the ‘beautiful, lush green vegetable garden’ sitting beside it, in which he and his team have been working with the farmers. Since the project began, farmers trained in saline agriculture have reported increases of two to three more harvests per year.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Greg Spotts from Los Angeles (LA) in the USA is chief sustainability officer of the city’s street services department. He leads the Cool Streets LA programme, a series of pilot projects, which include the planting of trees and the installation of a ‘cool pavement’ system, designed to help reach the city’s goal of bringing down its average temperature by 1.5°C. ‘Urban cooling is literally a matter of life and death for our future in LA,’ says Spotts. Using a Geographic Information System data mapping tool, the programme identified streets with low tree canopy cover in three of the city’s neighbourhoods and covered them with a light-grey, light-reflecting coating, which had already been shown to lower road surface temperature in Los Angeles by 6°C. Spotts says one of these streets, in the Winnetka neighbourhood of San Fernando Valley, can now be seen as a pale crescent, the only cool spot on an otherwise red thermal image, from the International Space Station.

questions.title: More work will need to be done in future decades to deal with the impact of rising water levels.
questions.analysis:
第一步：定位题干关键词。题干核心信息为“未来几十年需要做更多工作来应对海平面上升的影响”，可拆解为“More work will need to be done”“in future decades”“to deal with”“the impact of rising water levels”四个关键线索。
 第二步：回原文匹配人名及观点。依次查看各个人名所在段落：Section B提到Yanira Pineda，Section C提到Susanna Tol，Section D提到Elizabeth English，Section E提到Raisa Chowdhury，Section F提到Greg Spotts。重点关注是否有与题干线索相关的表述。
 第三步：锁定答案句。在Section B中，Yanira Pineda明确提到“‘We know that in 20, 30, 40 years, we'll need to go back in there and adjust to the changing environment,’ she says.”。其中，“in 20, 30, 40 years”对应“in future decades”，“we'll need to go back in there and adjust”对应“More work will need to be done”，“to adjust to”对应“to deal with”，“the changing environment”结合上下文（迈阿密海滩应对海水倒灌和洪水，即海平面上升的影响）对应“the impact of rising water levels”。所有线索均匹配，因此确定该句为答案句。

content.title: A new role for livestock guard dogs
content.summary: {'A': '数千年来，畜牧护卫犬协助牧羊人保护牲畜免受狼和熊等捕食者侵害，但在19 - 20世纪捕食者大量灭绝使护卫犬失业，近年来随着野生动物保护力度加大，捕食者数量回升，护卫犬意外复兴。', 'B': '如今全球约有50个品种的护卫犬在工作，它们从小与所守护的动物一起长大，会将自己置于牲畜和威胁之间，必要时驱赶捕食者，其初始训练是让它们明白牲畜是其生活重心。', 'C': '证据表明护卫犬非常有效，如葡萄牙超90%参与相关项目的农民认为其表现良好或优秀，澳大利亚超65%的牧民称有了护卫犬后捕食现象完全停止，其余大多也看到攻击减少。', 'D': '如今护卫犬有了新角色，即帮助保护捕食者，减少牲畜损失有望让农民对捕食者更宽容，减少捕杀，纳米比亚引入护卫犬后超90%的农民称牲畜损失大幅减少，也减少了对捕食者的捕杀。', 'E': '英国诺丁汉特伦特大学的贝瑟尼·史密斯发现，高达88%的农民称使用护卫犬后不再捕杀捕食者，但提醒要谨慎对待这种自我报告的结果，且护卫犬可能会将捕食者驱赶到未受保护的相邻农场。', 'F': '使用护卫犬可能存在意外的生态影响，减少一种捕食者的死亡可能对其他物种产生负面影响，如纳米比亚引入护卫犬后豺狼被杀数量增加，且护卫犬还会传播疾病、与其他食肉动物竞争食物、影响猎物行为。', 'G': '目前证据显示这些影响并非总是负面的，护卫犬能保护易受攻击的野生动物，澳大利亚研究人员正利用它们促进生物多样性，若运用得当，护卫犬在促进人类与野生动物和谐共存方面前景光明。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: For thousands of years, livestock guard dogs worked alongside shepherds to protect their sheep, goats and cattle from predators such as wolves and bears. But in the 19th and 20th centuries, when such predators were largely exterminated, most guard dogs lost their jobs. In recent years, however, as increased efforts have been made to protect wild animals, predators have become more widespread again. As a result, farmers once more need to protect their livestock, and guard dogs are enjoying an unexpected revival.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Today there are around 50 breeds of guard dogs on duty in various parts of the world. These dogs are raised from an early age with the animals they will be watching and eventually these animals become the dog’s family. The dogs will place themselves between the livestock and any threat, barking loudly. If necessary, they will chase away predators, but often their mere presence is sufficient. 'Their initial training is to make them understand that livestock is going to be their life,' says Dan Macon, a shepherd with three guard dogs. 'A fluffy white puppy is fun to be around, but too much human affection makes it a great dog for guarding the front porch, rather than a great livestock guard dog.'

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: The evidence indicates that guard dogs are highly effective. For example, in Portugal, biologist Silvia Ribeiro has found that more than 90 per cent of the farmers participating in a programme to train and use guard dogs to protect their herds against attack from wolves rate the performance of the dogs as very good or excellent. In a study carried out in Australia by Linda van Bommel and Chris Johnson at the University of Tasmania, more than 65 per cent of herders reported that predation stopped completely after they got the dogs, and almost all the rest saw a decrease in attacks. 'If they are managed and used properly, livestock guard dogs are the most efficient control method that we have in terms of the amount of livestock that they save from predation,' says van Bommel.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: But today’s guard dogs also have a new role – to help preserve the predators. It is hoped that reductions in livestock losses can make farmers more tolerant of predators and less likely to kill them. In Namibia, more than 90 per cent of cheetahs live outside protected areas, close to humans raising livestock. As a result, the cheetahs are often held responsible for animal losses, and large numbers have been killed by farmers. When guard dogs were introduced, more than 90 per cent of farmers reported a dramatic reduction in livestock losses, and said that as a result they were less likely to kill predators. Julie Young, at Utah State University in the US, believes this result applies widely. 'There is common ground from the livestock perspective and from the conservation perspective,' she says. 'If ranchers don’t have a dead cow, they will not make a call to apply for a permit to kill a wolf.'

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Looking at all the published evidence, Bethany Smith at Nottingham Trent University in the UK found that up to 88 per cent of farmers said they no longer killed predators after using dogs – but warned that such self-reported results must be taken with a pinch of salt. What's more, it is possible that livestock guard dogs merely displace predators to unprotected neighbouring properties, where their fate isn’t recorded. 'In some regions, we work with almost every farmer, but in others only one or two have dogs,' says Ribeiro. 'If we are not working with everybody, we are transferring the wolf pressure to the neighbour’s herd and he can use poison and kill an entire pack of wolves.'

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Another concern is whether there may be unintended ecological effects of using guard dogs. Studies suggest that reducing deaths of one type of predator may have a negative impact on other species. The extent of this problem isn’t known, but the consequences are clear in Namibia. Cheetahs aren’t the only species that cause sheep and goat losses there: other predators also attack livestock. In 2015, researchers reported that in spite of the impact farmers obtaining guard dogs had on cheetahs, the number of jackals killed by dogs and people actually increased. Guard dogs have other ecological impacts too. They have been found to spread diseases to wild animals, including endangered Ethiopian wolves. They may also compete with other carnivores for food. And by creating a 'landscape of fear', their mere presence can influence the behaviour of prey animals.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: The evidence so far, however, indicates that these consequences aren’t always negative. Guard dogs can deliver unexpected benefits by protecting vulnerable wildlife from predators. For example, their presence has been found to protect birds which build their nests on the ground in fields, where foxes would normally raid them. Indeed, Australian researchers are now using dogs to enhance biodiversity and create refuges for species threatened by predation. So if we can get this right, there may be a bright future for guard dogs in promoting harmonious coexistence between humans and wildlife.

questions.title: an example of how one predator has been protected by the introduction of livestock guard dogs
questions.analysis:
1. **题目定位**：题目要求找到“一个关于某种捕食者如何因引入牲畜护卫犬而得到保护的例子”。核心在于“example”（例子）、“one predator”（一种捕食者）、“has been protected”（得到保护）以及“by the introduction of livestock guard dogs”（通过引入牲畜护卫犬）。
 2. **关键词定位到原文**：
 * 带着“example”和“predator”、“protected”、“guard dogs introduced”这些关键词浏览各段。
 * 在D段，首先看到“In Namibia”，这是一个具体地点，暗示了一个具体案例（example）。
 * 接着出现了“cheetahs”，这是一种具体的捕食者（one predator）。
 * 随后提到“large numbers have been killed by farmers”，说明猎豹之前面临被杀害的威胁。
 * 关键句“When guard dogs were introduced”直接对应了“by the introduction of livestock guard dogs”。
 * 结果是“more than 90 per cent of farmers... said that as a result they were less likely to kill predators”，这表明农民因为引入了护卫犬而减少了对猎豹（捕食者）的杀害，即猎豹得到了保护（has been protected）。
 3. **寻找答案句**：综合上述线索，D段中从“In Namibia, more than 90 per cent of cheetahs...”开始，直至“... they were less likely to kill predators.”这一整部分内容，完整地构成了题目所要求的例子。
 4. **答案句理解**：该答案句清晰地叙述了在纳米比亚，猎豹因为捕食牲畜而被农民大量杀害。当引入护卫犬后，牲畜损失减少，农民因此不太可能再去杀害猎豹。这就直接说明了“一种捕食者（猎豹）如何因引入牲畜护卫犬而得到保护”，完全符合题干要求。

questions.title: a reference to how livestock guard dogs gain their skills
questions.analysis:
第一步：题目定位。题目要求找“提及牧羊犬如何获得技能”的段落，核心是“how...gain their skills”（如何获得技能），即需要寻找描述牧羊犬技能获取方式或过程的内容。
 第二步：关键词对应。题干中“how”对应原文描述方式的短语“are raised from an early age with”（从小共同生活）和“initial training”（初始训练）；“livestock guard dogs”对应原文主体“guard dogs”“livestock guard dog”；“gain”对应技能获取的动作“raised”（抚养）和“training is to make them understand”（训练使其明白）；“their skills”对应技能的具体内容“understand that livestock is going to be their life”（明白牲畜是生命核心）及通过对比“guarding the front porch...rather than...livestock guard dog”（区分看门与护畜技能）明确的技能方向。
 第三步：答案句理解。B段中“These dogs are raised from an early age with...”说明通过从小与牲畜共同生活，让狗将牲畜视为家人，形成保护本能；“Their initial training is to make them understand...”直接指出通过训练让狗明确守护牲畜是其核心任务。这两句完整解释了牧羊犬获得技能的具体方式，因此B段符合题目要求。

Questions 32–36
Look at the following statements and the list of people below.
Match each statement with the correct person, A – E.

questions.title: The use of guard dogs may save the lives of both livestock and wild animals.
questions.analysis:
1. **题干关键词拆解**：题干核心是“使用护卫犬（The use of guard dogs）可能同时（both...and...）拯救牲畜（livestock）和野生动物（wild animals）的生命（save the lives of）”。需要找到同时涉及“护卫犬使用”“拯救牲畜”“拯救野生动物”且体现“两者兼顾”逻辑的专家观点。
 2. **定位原文段落和人物**：通读全文，Section D提到“today’s guard dogs also have a new role – to help preserve the predators”（护卫犬帮助保护捕食者），并引用了Julie Young的观点，因此重点关注该部分。
 3. **匹配线索词**： 
 - “The use of guard dogs”对应原文“when guard dogs were introduced”（引入护卫犬时）； 
 - “save the lives of livestock”对应“ranchers don’t have a dead cow”（牧场主没有死牛，即牲畜存活）； 
 - “save the lives of wild animals”对应“not make a call to apply for a permit to kill a wolf”（不申请杀狼，即野生动物存活）； 
 - “both...and...”对应“common ground from the livestock perspective and from the conservation perspective”（牲畜角度和保护角度有共同点，即两者兼顾）。
 4. **理解答案句逻辑**：Julie Young的话先总述“牲畜和保护角度有共同点”，再用具体例子解释：护卫犬让牧场主“没有死牛”（拯救牲畜），因此“不申请杀狼”（拯救野生动物），完整匹配题干“同时拯救两者生命”的逻辑。

central_sentences.sentence: 'There is common ground from the livestock perspective and from the conservation perspective,' she says. 'If ranchers don’t have a dead cow, they will not make a call to apply for a permit to kill a wolf.'
central_sentences.translation: “从牲畜养殖的角度和动物保护的角度来看，存在共同点，”她说。“如果牧场主没有死牛，他们就不会打电话申请许可证去杀狼。”

questions.analysis:
第一步：明确题干核心。题干意为“那些声称在使用牧羊犬后行为有所改变的说法可能不完全准确”，关键在于“行为改变的声称”和“可能不准确”这两个信息点。
 第二步：定位原文段落。带着核心信息浏览文章，发现E段提到“Bethany Smith... found that up to 88 per cent of farmers said they no longer killed predators after using dogs – but warned that such self-reported results must be taken with a pinch of salt”，这里的“farmers said they no longer killed predators”对应“行为改变的声称”，“must be taken with a pinch of salt”（持保留态度）对应“可能不准确”。
 第三步：匹配线索词。题干中“Claims of a change in behaviour”对应“farmers said they no longer killed predators”，“from those using livestock guard dogs”对应“farmers ... after using dogs”，“may not be totally accurate”对应“must be taken with a pinch of salt”，“Claims”对应“self-reported results”，所有线索词均匹配。
 第四步：确认人物。该观点明确由“Bethany Smith”提出，因此答案为E。

questions.title: Teaching a livestock guard dog how to do its work needs a different focus from teaching a house guard dog.
questions.analysis:
第一步：定位题干关键词。题干核心是“训练牧场护卫犬和家养护卫犬需要不同侧重点”，关键线索词包括“Teaching”“how to do its work”“a different focus from”“a livestock guard dog”“a house guard dog”。
 第二步：寻找对应人名段落。在文章中依次查找选项中人物出现的段落，发现Dan Macon出现在Section B。
 第三步：匹配线索词对。在Section B中，Dan Macon提到“initial training”对应题干“Teaching”；“to make them understand that livestock is going to be their life”对应“how to do its work”；“rather than”明确体现“a different focus from”；“a great livestock guard dog”直接对应“a livestock guard dog”；“a great dog for guarding the front porch”（守护前门廊的狗）即“a house guard dog”。
 第四步：确认答案句逻辑。Dan Macon的话通过“过多人类喜爱会让狗成为看家犬而非牧场护卫犬”，清晰对比了两种训练的不同侧重点，与题干完全匹配。

central_sentences.sentence: 'Their initial training is to make them understand that livestock is going to be their life,' says Dan Macon, a shepherd with three guard dogs. 'A fluffy white puppy is fun to be around, but too much human affection makes it a great dog for guarding the front porch, rather than a great livestock guard dog.'
central_sentences.translation: “它们最初的训练是让它们明白牲畜将是它们的生活重心，”拥有三只护卫犬的牧羊人丹·梅肯（Dan Macon）说。“一只毛茸茸的小白狗在身边很有趣，但过多的人类喜爱会让它成为一只很棒的看家护院犬（守护前门廊的狗），而不是一只优秀的牧场护卫犬。”

groups.title: Unintended ecological effects of using guard dogs
groups.desc:
Questions 37–40
Complete the summary below.
Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
groups.collect: In Namibia, livestock guard dogs have been used to protect domestic animals from attacks by cheetahs. This has led to a rise in the deaths of other predators, particularly $40093$. In addition, it has been suggested that the dogs could have $40094$ which may affect other species, and that they may reduce the amount of $40095$ available to certain wild animals. On the other hand, these dogs may help birds by protecting their nests. These might otherwise be threatened by predators such as $40096$.

questions.analysis:
1. **题目定位**：题干提到"In Namibia"（在纳米比亚）和"rise in the deaths of other predators"（其他捕食者死亡数量上升），结合题目主题"Unintended ecological effects"（意外生态影响），可定位到文章Section F（讨论护卫犬的意外生态影响）。 
2. **寻找答案句**：在Section F中，先提到"the consequences are clear in Namibia"（在纳米比亚，后果很明显），随后具体说明"Cheetahs aren’t the only species... other predators also attack livestock"（猎豹不是唯一的物种……其他捕食者也攻击家畜），接着给出研究结果："the number of jackals killed by dogs and people actually increased"（被狗和人类杀死的豺的数量实际上增加了），此句直接对应题干"led to a rise in the deaths of other predators"。 
3. **答案句理解**：题干中"particularly"（尤其是）提示需要具体的捕食者名称，答案句中"jackals"（豺）是"other predators"（其他捕食者）的具体例子，且"number...killed...increased"（被杀死的数量增加）对应"rise in the deaths"（死亡数量上升），因此答案为"jackals"。

1. **题目定位**：题干讨论“护卫犬帮助鸟类保护巢穴，否则巢穴会被某种捕食者威胁”，属于护卫犬的正面生态影响。根据题目顺序，前文37-39题对应F段（负面生态影响），故40题应在F段之后的G段（正面影响）寻找。
 2. **线索词对应**：在G段中，“On the other hand”与“however”（表转折，引出正面影响）对应；“these dogs”与“their presence”（their指代guard dogs）对应；“help birds by protecting their nests”与“protect birds which build their nests”对应；“threatened by predators”与“raid them”（raid意为“袭击”，them指代nests，即捕食者袭击巢穴）对应；“such as”与“where”（where引导从句举例说明具体捕食者）对应。
